new_value: {"description":"Fix review finding #9 by enforcing strict iteration parsing bounds, rejecting malformed server-first/server-final fields, and escaping username in SCRAM client-first per RFC 5802 (= becomes =3D, comma becomes =2C). Add unit tests for malicious and invalid inputs."}
old_value: {"id":"pgsql-uja.9","title":"Tighten SCRAM parsing and username escaping for RFC-compliant auth hardening","description":"Fix review finding #9 by enforcing strict iteration parsing bounds, rejecting malformed server-first/server-final fields, and escaping username in SCRAM client-first per RFC 5802 ( -\u003e ,  -\u003e ). Add unit tests for malicious/invalid inputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T11:00:34Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-05T11:00:34Z"}

{"id":"pgsql-uja.1","title":"Serialize per-client protocol operations to prevent concurrent socket/decoder interleaving","description":"Fix review finding #1 by enforcing single in-flight protocol operation per Client/Connection across query, prepared statements, stream, COPY, and notification wait APIs. Add an internal operation guard (acquire/release with error-safe release), ensure stream/copy long-lived flows hold exclusivity correctly, and add concurrency regression tests that previously corrupted protocol flow.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T10:59:49Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-05T10:59:49Z"}

{"id":"pgsql-uja.5","title":"Fix pool semaphore permit leak when connect fails after acquire","description":"Fix review finding #4: ensure Pool::acquire always releases acquired semaphore permit if creating/replacing a client fails. Add regression coverage proving capacity is preserved after repeated failed connect attempts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T11:00:09Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-05T11:00:09Z"}

new_value: Fixed permit release on connect failure and added pool_wbtest regression

old_value: {"id":"pgsql-uja.6","title":"Make notification handling FIFO and batch-safe","description":"Fix review finding #5 by replacing LIFO pop behavior and eliminating early-return batch drops in wait_for_notification. Guarantee FIFO ordering for queued notifications and preserve all notifications parsed in a receive batch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"gbikal@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T11:00:13Z","created_by":"Bikal Gurung","updated_at":"2026-03-05T11:00:13Z"}

new_value: Switched notification dequeue to FIFO and made wait_for_notification batch-safe via enqueue+post-batch dequeue

{"notes":"Implemented per-connection operation_guard semaphore and routed query/prepare/bind_execute/copy_in/copy_out/wait_for_notification/prepared close/stream lifecycle through serialized access. Stream now holds guard across active portal lifetime and releases deterministically on exhaustion/error/close. Need dedicated concurrency integration validation before closing."}
